paper_title: Image Processing and Analysis in P. tricornutum Cells

methods.description: This method involves processing fluorescence images to measure the mean fluorescence amplitude within a region of interest (ROI) over time for each cell. It includes normalizing fluorescence traces and generating pseudocolored images to visualize Ca 2+ elevations.

steps.description: Process images using NIS-ELEMENTS v.3.1 software to measure mean fluorescence amplitude within a region of interest (ROI) over time for each cell.
steps.searchable_summary: Use NIS-ELEMENTS software to measure fluorescence amplitude in ROI over time

steps.description: Normalize fluorescence amplitude of R-GECO1 by dividing each trace by the initial value (F / F0).
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize fluorescence traces by initial value

steps.description: Perform background subtraction for PtR1-GFP dual imaging setup.
steps.searchable_summary: Subtract background fluorescence for dual imaging

steps.description: Generate pseudocolored images representing the change in fluorescence (F / F0) using IMAGEJ software.

methods.description: This method describes the procedure for imaging P. tricornutum cells under hypo-osmotic shock conditions using epifluorescence microscopy. It includes the preparation of cells and the delivery of rapid and gradual shifts in osmolarity.

steps.description: Grow P. tricornutum cells in liquid culture for 72 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Cultivate P. tricornutum cells for 72 hours

steps.description: Deliver rapid shifts in hypo-osmotic conditions by switching perfusion from ASW to ASW diluted with distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: Switch perfusion to induce hypo-osmotic shock

steps.description: Preperfuse cells with Ca 2+ -free medium (+200 µM EGTA) for at least 5 minutes.
Generate gradual shifts in osmolarity using a gradient mixer with a main chamber and a feeding chamber.
steps.searchable_summary: Use gradient mixer to create osmolarity gradient
